source: Official Path of Exile patch notes and official gather nodes

Definition.text: Tailwind is an aura-type buff that grants a speed modifier and scales with recent skill usage through Gathering Winds.

Purpose.text: Increase movement, cast, attack, and Throw/skill use rates for the wearer and nearby allies.

Core Rules.text: Gathering Winds grants the Tailwind aura to you and nearby Allies. In 3.2, Tailwind granted 1000 Evasion, 10% increased Action Speed, and 10% increased Effect of Tailwind per skill used recently, up to 100%.

Internal Rules.text: Tailwind effect is tied to the active source that grants it (for Deadeye this is Gathering Winds) and is further modified by Gale Force terms from the same notable.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Source grants Tailwind aura state; 2) base action-speed bonus applies; 3) Effect of Tailwind is multiplied by recent skill-use scaling (per-stack term); 4) final action-speed result is used by downstream action-time systems.

Priority Rules.text: Patch-level replacement of the per-stack term in Gathering Winds (3.25: 10% from 15%) applies before computing final Tailwind action-speed effect; earlier patch values do not remain active when superseded.

Preconditions.text: Tailwind requires a valid source that applies Tailwind to the entity (for Deadeye, Gathering Winds).

Postconditions.text: Entity and nearby allies receive Tailwind action-speed scaling for the active buff period and gain the listed nearby bonus context from the source.

Limitations.text: Tailwind is a buff context effect; it is not a direct Hit modifier and does not replace base action-speed modifiers from other systems.

Caps.text: In 3.2 wording, increased effect scaling is capped at 100%.

Formula.text: Let n be recent-use stacks used by Gathering Winds and c be the per-stack bonus term. In 3.2, Base ActionSpeed = 10% and effect scale is c=10% per stack. 3.25 changed c to 10% from 15%. Final ActionSpeed = 10% * (1 + min(1, n * c_effect)), with n capped by the source definition.

prevents: Using outdated patch constants after a patch-time replacement of per-stack term

Conflicts.text: Stacking Tailwind instances from the same source is replaced by strongest/most-recent behavior rather than additive independent aura reapplication.

Exceptions.text: A patch-level bug fix may alter Tailwind source precedence for sources that share mechanics; this must be recorded as separate patch entries.

Hidden Rules.text: Different patch contexts can use distinct scaling definitions; model Tailwind strictly per patch timeline to avoid mixing historical values.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Tailwind as an action-speed aura with explicit per-patch scaling terms; avoid combining 3.2 and 3.25 formulas unless continuity is explicitly stated.

Historical Changes.text: 3.2 introduced Tailwind via Gathering Winds. 3.25 replaced the per-Gale Force term from 15% to 10%.

text.note: Added Gathering Winds with Tailwind aura; 10% increased action speed and 10% increased effect of Tailwind per skill used recently up to 100%.
Gathering Winds now provides 10% increased Effect of Tailwind per Gale Force (previously 15%).

text: In 3.2 form, Tailwind with 10 recent recent-skill units reaches the documented cap.
In 3.25+ form, per-unit effect uses the new 10% term for Gale Force scaling.
Nearby Allies receiving Tailwind inherit the aura context from source rules.
Treating Tailwind as a fixed-value status not affected by patch-level per-stack terms.
Assuming the 3.2 and 3.25 formulas are interchangeable without timeline checks.
